Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
description: Israel now controls 18% of the occupied territories, compared to 7% before the war in Gaza. A report from the non-profit organizations Peace Now and Kerem Navot has been documenting the acceleration of land grabs, the construction of settler-only roads and the expulsion of local communities over the past three and a half years.

headline: French parliamentary inquiry slams government's handling of British border

headline: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
description: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.

headline: Five questions on Marine Le Pen's appeal to France's highest court
description: Hours after being convicted, the far-right leader said she would run for president in 2027 and campaign without wearing an electronic monitor. Here's what that claim would actually require.

headline: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
description: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

headline: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
description: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.
